question: Which player's name has a high school score of at least 1000 and was successfully selected as a goalie during tryouts?
gold_sql: SELECT Player.pName FROM Player JOIN Tryout ON Player.pID = Tryout.pID WHERE Player.HS >= 1000 AND Tryout.pPos = 'goalie' AND Tryout.decision = 'yes';
pred_sql: SELECT p.pName FROM Player AS p JOIN Tryout AS t ON p.pID = t.pID WHERE t.cName = 'Goalie' AND t.decision = 'Yes' AND p.HS >= 1000;
raw_prediction: {"SQL": "SELECT p.pName FROM Player AS p JOIN Tryout AS t ON p.pID = t.pID WHERE t.cName = 'Goalie' AND t.decision = 'Yes' AND p.HS >= 1000;"}

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT p.pName FROM Player AS p JOIN Tryout AS t ON p.pID = t.pID WHERE t.cName = 'Goalie' AND t.decision = 'Yes' AND p.HS >= 1000;

question: Find the average millisecond length of Latin and Pop tracks.
gold_sql: SELECT AVG(Milliseconds) FROM GENRE AS T1 JOIN TRACK AS T2 ON T1.GenreId  =  T2.GenreId WHERE T1.Name  =  "Latin" OR T1.Name  =  "Pop"
pred_sql: SELECT AVG(T2.Milliseconds) FROM GENRE AS T1 JOIN TRACK AS T2 ON T1.GenreID = T2.GenreID WHERE T1.Name IN ('Latin', 'Pop');
raw_prediction: {"SQL": "SELECT AVG(T2.Milliseconds) FROM GENRE AS T1 JOIN TRACK AS T2 ON T1.GenreID = T2.GenreID WHERE T1.Name IN ('Latin', 'Pop');"}

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT AVG(T2.Milliseconds) FROM GENRE AS T1 JOIN TRACK AS T2 ON T1.GenreID = T2.GenreID WHERE T1.Name IN ('Latin', 'Pop');

question: How many distinct male and female staff members worked in the department with ID 5?
gold_sql: SELECT staff_gender, COUNT(DISTINCT s.staff_id) FROM Staff s JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments sa ON s.staff_id = sa.staff_id WHERE sa.department_id = 5 GROUP BY staff_gender;
pred_sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT s.staff_id) FROM Staff s JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments sd ON s.staff_id = sd.staff_id WHERE sd.department_id = 5 AND s.staff_gender IN ('M', 'F');
raw_prediction: {"SQL": "SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT s.staff_id) FROM Staff s JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments sd ON s.staff_id = sd.staff_id WHERE sd.department_id = 5 AND s.staff_gender IN ('M', 'F');"}

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT s.staff_id) FROM Staff s JOIN Staff_Department_Assignments sd ON s.staff_id = sd.staff_id WHERE sd.department_id = 5 AND s.staff_gender IN ('M', 'F');

question: What concerts occurred at the stadium with the highest capacity and what was their theme?
gold_sql: SELECT c.concert_Name, c.Theme FROM concert AS c JOIN stadium AS s ON c.Stadium_ID = s.Stadium_ID WHERE s.Capacity = (SELECT MAX(Capacity) FROM stadium);
pred_sql: SELECT c.concert_Name, s.Name FROM concert AS c JOIN stadium AS s ON c.Stadium_ID = s.Stadium_ID WHERE s.Capacity = (SELECT MAX(Capacity) FROM stadium);
raw_prediction: {"SQL": "SELECT c.concert_Name, s.Name FROM concert AS c JOIN stadium AS s ON c.Stadium_ID = s.Stadium_ID WHERE s.Capacity = (SELECT MAX(Capacity) FROM stadium);"}

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT c.concert_Name, s.Name FROM concert AS c JOIN stadium AS s ON c.Stadium_ID = s.Stadium_ID WHERE s.Capacity = (SELECT MAX(Capacity) FROM stadium);

question: Which city has hosted the highest number of farm competitions, and how many have they hosted?
gold_sql: SELECT city.Official_Name, COUNT(farm_competition.Competition_ID) AS Num_Hosted FROM city JOIN farm_competition ON city.City_ID = farm_competition.Host_city_ID GROUP BY city.Official_Name ORDER BY Num_Hosted DESC LIMIT 1;
pred_sql: SELECT c.Official_Name , COUNT(fc.Host_city_ID) FROM city AS c JOIN farm_competition AS fc ON c.City_ID = fc.Host_city_ID GROUP BY c.City_ID ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1;
raw_prediction: {"SQL": "SELECT c.Official_Name , COUNT(fc.Host_city_ID) FROM city AS c JOIN farm_competition AS fc ON c.City_ID = fc.Host_city_ID GROUP BY c.City_ID ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1;"}

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT c.Official_Name , COUNT(fc.Host_city_ID) FROM city AS c JOIN farm_competition AS fc ON c.City_ID = fc.Host_city_ID GROUP BY c.City_ID ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1;

question: What is the name of the customer(s) whose order placed in the year 1974 currently has the packing status?
gold_sql: SELECT customer_name FROM Customers WHERE customer_id IN (SELECT customer_id FROM Orders WHERE order_status = 'Packing' AND date_order_placed LIKE '1974%')

raw_prediction: {"SQL": "SELECT c.customer_name FROM Customers AS c JOIN Orders AS o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.order_status = 'Packing' AND YEAR(o.date_order_placed) = 1974;"}

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT c.customer_name FROM Customers AS c JOIN Orders AS o ON c.customer_id = o.customer_id WHERE o.order_status = 'Packing' AND YEAR(o.date_order_placed) = 1974;

question: How many total cities were affected by storms which caused more than 5 deaths?
gold_sql: SELECT SUM(ar.Number_city_affected) AS total_cities_affected FROM storm s JOIN affected_region ar ON s.Storm_ID = ar.Storm_ID WHERE s.Number_Deaths > 5;
pred_sql: SELECT SUM(affected_region.Number_city_affected) FROM storm JOIN affected_region ON storm.Storm_ID = affected_region.Storm_ID WHERE storm.Damage_millions_USD > 5;

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT SUM(affected_region.Number_city_affected) FROM storm JOIN affected_region ON storm.Storm_ID = affected_region.Storm_ID WHERE storm.Damage_millions_USD > 5;

question: Which team selected the player from the 2007 MLS SuperDraft, and what was their playing position? Also, provide details about any players from the same team in the player table, including their years played and their total win-loss record.
gold_sql: SELECT ms.Team, ms.Player, ms.Position, p.Years_Played, p.Total_WL FROM match_season AS ms JOIN player AS p ON ms.Team = p.Team WHERE ms.Draft_Class = '2007 MLS SuperDraft';
pred_sql: SELECT p.Player, p.Position FROM player AS p JOIN match_season AS ms ON p.Team = ms.Team WHERE ms.Year = '2007' AND ms.Position = 'D';
raw_prediction: {"SQL": "SELECT p.Player, p.Position FROM player AS p JOIN match_season AS ms ON p.Team = ms.Team WHERE ms.Year = '2007' AND ms.Position = 'D';"}

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT p.Player, p.Position FROM player AS p JOIN match_season AS ms ON p.Team = ms.Team WHERE ms.Year = '2007' AND ms.Position = 'D';

question: Which visitors with a membership level greater than 1 purchased more than 5 tickets in a single visit?

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT v.Name FROM visitor v JOIN visit vi ON v.ID = vi.visitor_ID WHERE v.Level_of_membership > 1 AND vi.Num_of_Ticket > 5;

question: What is the average share percentage for channels broadcasting programs during the night?

question: What campus started in year 1956, has more than 200 full time students, and more than 400 students enrolled?
gold_sql: SELECT T1.campus FROM campuses AS t1 JOIN enrollments AS t2 ON t1.id  =  t2.campus WHERE t2.year  =  1956 AND totalenrollment_ay  >  400 AND FTE_AY  >  200

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT c.Campus FROM Campuses c JOIN Enrollments e ON c.Id = e.Campus WHERE c.Year = 1956 AND e.TotalEnrollment_AY > 200 AND e.FTE_AY > 400;

question: What is the least popular kind of decor?

question: Which student has the highest number of enrolments, and what is the count of their enrolments?
gold_sql: SELECT s.first_name, s.last_name, COUNT(se.student_enrolment_id) AS total_enrolments FROM Students s JOIN Student_Enrolment se ON s.student_id = se.student_id GROUP BY s.student_id ORDER BY total_enrolments DESC LIMIT 1;
pred_sql: SELECT S.first_name, S.last_name, COUNT(SE.student_enrolment_id) AS enrollment_count FROM Students S JOIN Student_Enrolment SE ON S.student_id = SE.student_id GROUP BY S.student_id ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC LIMIT 1;
raw_prediction: {"SQL": "SELECT S.first_name, S.last_name, COUNT(SE.student_enrolment_id) AS enrollment_count FROM Students S JOIN Student_Enrolment SE ON S.student_id = SE.student_id GROUP BY S.student_id ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC LIMIT 1;"}

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT S.first_name, S.last_name, COUNT(SE.student_enrolment_id) AS enrollment_count FROM Students S JOIN Student_Enrolment SE ON S.student_id = SE.student_id GROUP BY S.student_id ORDER BY enrollment_count DESC LIMIT 1;

question: What is the average population of counties whose zip codes correspond to election districts represented by delegates first elected before the year 2000?
gold_sql: SELECT AVG(c.Population) FROM county c JOIN election e ON c.Zip_code = CONCAT('D', LPAD(e.District, 2, '0')) WHERE e.First_Elected < 2000;
pred_sql: SELECT AVG(county.Population) FROM county JOIN election ON county.Zip_code = election.District WHERE election.First_Elected < 2000;
raw_prediction: {"SQL": "SELECT AVG(county.Population) FROM county JOIN election ON county.Zip_code = election.District WHERE election.First_Elected < 2000;"}

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT AVG(county.Population) FROM county JOIN election ON county.Zip_code = election.District WHERE election.First_Elected < 2000;

question: How many CV-type documents were stored on dates that fall on day number 7?

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT cd.Document_ID) FROM All_Documents cd JOIN Ref_Calendar rc ON cd.Date_Stored = rc.Calendar_Date WHERE cd.Document_Type_Code = 'CV' AND rc.Day_Number = 7;

question: What is the total amount of refunds for all invoices generated between March 1, 2018, and March 15, 2018?
gold_sql: SELECT SUM(transaction_amount) FROM Financial_Transactions WHERE transaction_type = 'Refund' AND invoice_number IN (SELECT invoice_number FROM Invoices WHERE invoice_date BETWEEN '2018-03-01' AND '2018-03-15');

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT SUM(ft.transaction_amount) FROM Financial_Transactions ft JOIN Invoices i ON ft.invoice_number = i.invoice_number WHERE ft.transaction_type = 'refund' AND i.invoice_date BETWEEN '2018-03-01' AND '2018-03-15';

question: Who does Iron Man follow that has more than 1 million followers?
gold_sql: SELECT name FROM user_profiles WHERE uid IN (SELECT f2 FROM follows WHERE f1 IN (SELECT uid FROM user_profiles WHERE name = 'Iron Man')) AND followers > 1000000;

question: Give the building that the instructor who teaches the greatest number of courses lives in.
gold_sql: SELECT T2.Building FROM COURSE AS T1 JOIN FACULTY AS T2 ON T1.Instructor  =  T2.FacID GROUP BY T1.Instructor ORDER BY count(*) DESC LIMIT 1

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT b.Building FROM Faculty f JOIN Course c ON f.FacID = c.Instructor WHERE f.Rank = (SELECT MAX(Rank) FROM Faculty);

question: Find the names of couples and the churches where the marriage took place, for all weddings held at churches organized in or before 1835. Also give the opening date of these churches.
gold_sql: SELECT p1.Name AS Groom, p2.Name AS Bride, c.Name AS Church_Name, c.Open_Date FROM wedding w JOIN people p1 ON w.Male_ID = p1.People_ID JOIN people p2 ON w.Female_ID = p2.People_ID JOIN church c ON w.Church_ID = c.Church_ID WHERE c.Open_Date <= 1835;
pred_sql: SELECT p.Name, c.Name FROM people p JOIN church c ON p.People_ID = c.Church_ID WHERE c.Organized_by IN (SELECT Church_ID FROM church WHERE Open_Date <= '1835-01-01') AND c.Church_ID IN (SELECT Church_ID FROM wedding WHERE Year >= 1835);
raw_prediction: {"SQL": "SELECT p.Name, c.Name FROM people p JOIN church c ON p.People_ID = c.Church_ID WHERE c.Organized_by IN (SELECT Church_ID FROM church WHERE Open_Date <= '1835-01-01') AND c.Church_ID IN (SELECT Church_ID FROM wedding WHERE Year >= 1835);"}

parsed_json.SQL: SELECT p.Name, c.Name FROM people p JOIN church c ON p.People_ID = c.Church_ID WHERE c.Organized_by IN (SELECT Church_ID FROM church WHERE Open_Date <= '1835-01-01') AND c.Church_ID IN (SELECT Church_ID FROM wedding WHERE Year >= 1835);

question: List the names of FDA-approved medicines that act as activitors in enzyme interactions.
gold_sql: SELECT m.name FROM medicine m JOIN medicine_enzyme_interaction mei ON m.id = mei.medicine_id WHERE mei.interaction_type = 'activitor' AND m.FDA_approved = 'Yes';
